title: Sliding Window Maximum Sum
code:
def max_sum_subarray(nums: list[int], k: int) -> int:
    window = sum(nums[:k])
    best = window
    for i in range(k, len(nums)):
        window += nums[i] - nums[i - k]
        best = max(best, window)
    return best
description: The sliding window technique maintains a running sum by adding the incoming element and subtracting the outgoing one, avoiding recomputing the full window each step. Reduces brute-force **O(nk)** to **O(n)**.
